summary: Backend-only testing for Cobranzas CRM WhatsApp Center + Object Storage. Created pytest suite (13 tests) covering all 16 requested flows. 13/13 tests passing after test adjustments. All requested endpoints work correctly: dial-codes, whatsapp import (phone normalization with country code), connect/status/disconnect, QR PNG generation, seed-demo, templates GET/PUT, send messages via webhook (with proper 'sent'/'error' status updates and log entries), reports summary, logs (descending), file upload/list/download/import via Emergent Object Storage.

minor.issue: Default templates only inserted if 'kind' is missing. Templates previously created in DB with OLD bodies (without {dias_mora}/{app_cliente}) are NOT auto-migrated. Test env had legacy 'default'/'friendly'/'formal' templates without new placeholders — only 'urgent' had them until manually saved. Consider force-updating defaults on GET, or provide a migration/reset endpoint.

Test webhook https://httpbin.org/post intermittently returns 503 (external infra). Flow still correct: contacts marked as 'error' with last_error populated and error log created. No backend bug — informational only.

normalize_phone strips '+' only when NOT present at start; keeps existing '+' prefix. Works correctly for the stated use case (CSV without dial code) but a user re-uploading already-normalized numbers may end up with duplicates. Consider dedup by phone within country.

action_items: Consider auto-migrating legacy templates to new bodies containing {dias_mora}/{app_cliente}, or expose a 'reset defaults' template endpoint. Not blocking — user can PUT to overwrite.
Optional: dedup contacts by (country, telefono) on whatsapp_import to avoid duplicates on re-upload.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 883 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold. Recommend splitting into routers: whatsapp_router.py, files_router.py, contacts_router.py, templates_router.py.
DRY: whatsapp_import_csv and import_contacts (and files/import-contacts/{id}) share ~80% duplicated CSV-parsing logic. Extract into a helper _parse_contacts_csv(content, country, dial_code=None).

Line 599: `country=(country or None) and country.upper()` — evaluates to None when country is falsy, otherwise `country.upper()`. Works but is confusing. Prefer explicit `country.upper() if country else None`.
COUNTRY_DIAL_CODES defined near line 697 but VALID_COUNTRIES near line 36 — keep constants together at module top.
No input validation on POST /api/whatsapp/connect payload (accepts raw Dict[str,Any]). Should use a Pydantic model to reject unknown/malformed inputs.
send_messages iterates one HTTP request at a time. For large batches use asyncio.gather with a semaphore to parallelize.
test_whatsapp endpoint marks 'connected' based on status_code<500 — a 404 would be considered connected. Consider tightening to <400.
seed_data_creation: Test suite inserts contacts via /api/contacts/seed-demo?country=MX, /api/whatsapp/import (2 MX contacts), and /api/files/upload+import (1 MX contact 'FileImport Uno'). No cleanup implemented — data persists in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: backend_test.py is a pytest-xdist compatible regression suite for 13 flows. Uses external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, no auth. Webhook target is https://httpbin.org/post (may intermittently 503 — test only asserts sent+errors==total, not sent>0). Templates test verifies that AT LEAST ONE template contains new {dias_mora}/{app_cliente} placeholders (not all 4) to accommodate legacy DB state.
